calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Victoria Robinson, Blake Cunningham And Maite Kvarna; Exhibits
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

People's motion for entry of default judgment that the $4,223.00 in U.S. currency is forfeited is granted. Judgment shall be entered providing for the forfeiture of the currency and the currency shall be distributed as provided in the Health and Safety Code. No opposition filed and the People have established a prima facie case for forfeiture.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment As To Claimant Brandon Jackson-Knight
Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Default Judgment As To Claimant Brandon Jackson-Knight.

Petitioner The People of the State of California's unopposed Motion For Default Judgment As To Claimant Brandon Jackson-Knight is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Petitioner moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 437(c) "on the grounds that there are no triable issues of material fact and that there is no defense to the action." There is no section 437, subdivision, so the reference must be to section 437c, the statute governing motions for summary judgment and summary adjudication. This conclusion is confirmed by the quoted text. Which sets out the standard for a motion for summary judgment by a petitioner or plaintiff. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivisions (a)(1) and (c).) Such motions must be filed at least 81 days before the hearing date. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivisions (a)(2).) The court cannot shorten he notice period. This motion was filed less than 60 days before the hearing date. Further, the moving part must submit a separate statement. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivisions (b)(2).) Courts rely heavily on separate statements. Petitioner here did not file a separate statement. The authority identified in the notice of motion controls. For defaults and default judgments, the court draws Petitioner's attention to Code of Civil Procedure section 585 et seq and SFLR 8.8. A motion noticed under section 585 and SFLR 8.8 may be filed and served consistent with Code of Civil Procedure section 1005, subdivisions (b).

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
